Package com.wip.controller

Class ParcelUiController

java.lang.Object
com.wip.controller.ParcelUiController
@Controller @RequestMapping("/parcels") public class ParcelUiController extends Object
Thymeleaf UI controller for managing parcel bookings in the Courier Tracking System.

Handles browser-facing HTTP requests under the /parcels path and renders server-side HTML views via Thymeleaf. This controller covers the full parcel lifecycle — listing all parcels, filtering by customer, creating new bookings, viewing details, editing, and deleting parcel records.

Security-aware: for non-admin users, the customer ID is automatically resolved from the authenticated Spring Security principal (CustomUserDetails) so that regular customers can only create and update parcels linked to their own account.

Page flow overview:

  • GET /parcels → parcel/list — all parcels
  • GET /parcels/by-customer/{customerId} → parcel/by-customer — parcels for one customer
  • GET /parcels/new → parcel/form — blank creation form
  • POST /parcels/save → redirect to details or re-render form on error
  • GET /parcels/{id} → parcel/details — single parcel view
  • GET /parcels/{id}/edit → parcel/form — pre-filled edit form
  • POST /parcels/{id}/update → redirect to details or re-render form on error
  • POST /parcels/{id}/delete → redirect to list
Since:
1.0
Version:
1.0
Author:
Dharshan K S (dharshan.ks@wipro.com)

  • Constructor Details

    • ParcelUiController

      public ParcelUiController(ParcelService parcelService)
      Constructs a ParcelUiController with the required ParcelService.
      Parameters:
      parcelService - the service bean responsible for parcel business logic; injected by Spring's dependency injection container

  • Method Details

    • listParcels

      @GetMapping public String listParcels(org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays the list of all parcel bookings in the system.

      Fetches every parcel record and adds them to the model as a parcels attribute before rendering the list view.

      Parameters:
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass the parcels attribute (a list of ParcelDto) to the view
      Returns:
      the logical view name "parcel/list"

    • listParcelsByCustomer

      @GetMapping("/by-customer/{customerId}") public String listParcelsByCustomer(@PathVariable Long customerId, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays the list of parcel bookings belonging to a specific customer.

      Fetches parcels filtered by the given customerId and renders them in the by-customer view. Both the parcel list and the customer ID are added to the model for display and navigation purposes.

      Parameters:
      customerId - the unique identifier of the customer whose parcels should be listed
      model - the Spring MVC Model; receives parcels (list of ParcelDto) and customerId attributes
      Returns:
      the logical view name "parcel/by-customer"

    • showCreateForm

      @GetMapping("/new") public String showCreateForm(@RequestParam(value="customerId",required=false) Long customerId, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays a blank form for creating a new parcel booking.

      For regular (non-admin) users, the customer ID is automatically populated from the authenticated Spring Security principal so that the user cannot create parcels on behalf of another customer. Admin users may optionally pre-populate the customer ID via the customerId request parameter. The current date is also added to the model to support date-picker defaults in the form.

      Parameters:
      customerId - an optional customer ID to pre-fill on the form; used by admins when navigating from a customer's detail page (may be null)
      model - the Spring MVC Model; receives parcelDto (an empty ParcelDto) and today (LocalDate) attributes
      Returns:
      the logical view name "parcel/form"

    • saveParcel

      @PostMapping("/save") public String saveParcel(@Valid @ModelAttribute("parcelDto") @Valid ParcelDto parcelDto, org.springframework.validation.BindingResult bindingResult, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Processes the submission of the new parcel creation form and persists the booking.

      For non-admin users, the customer ID is re-enforced from the authenticated Spring Security principal to prevent tampering with hidden form fields. Bean Validation is then applied; on failure the form is re-rendered with inline error messages. On successful persistence, the user is redirected to the new parcel's detail page.

      Handles ResourceNotFoundException (e.g., customer not found or user has no linked customer profile) and generic exceptions, adding descriptive error messages to the model in both cases.

      Parameters:
      parcelDto - the form-bound parcel data submitted by the user; must satisfy all Bean Validation constraints
      bindingResult - holds validation errors produced during binding and validation
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error messages and the today date attribute back to the view
      Returns:
      a redirect to /parcels/{id} on success, or the logical view name "parcel/form" when validation or persistence fails

    • parcelDetails

      @GetMapping("/{id}") public String parcelDetails(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays the detail view for a single parcel booking identified by its ID.

      Fetches the parcel by id and adds it as a parcel model attribute. If the parcel does not exist, the service layer propagates a ResourceNotFoundException.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the parcel whose details are to be displayed
      model - the Spring MVC Model; receives the parcel attribute (a ParcelDto) for rendering
      Returns:
      the logical view name "parcel/details"
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no parcel exists with the given ID

    • editParcelForm

      @GetMapping("/{id}/edit") public String editParcelForm(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Displays a pre-populated form for editing an existing parcel booking.

      Loads the current parcel data by id and binds it to the parcelDto model attribute so that all existing field values appear pre-filled in the form. The current date is also provided to support date-picker defaults.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the parcel to be edited
      model - the Spring MVC Model; receives parcelDto (pre-filled ParcelDto) and today (LocalDate) attributes
      Returns:
      the logical view name "parcel/form"
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no parcel exists with the given ID

    • updateParcel

      @PostMapping("/{id}/update") public String updateParcel(@PathVariable Long id, @Valid @ModelAttribute("parcelDto") @Valid ParcelDto parcelDto, org.springframework.validation.BindingResult bindingResult, org.springframework.ui.Model model)
      Processes the submission of the parcel edit form and persists the updated booking details.

      For non-admin users, the customer ID is re-enforced from the authenticated Spring Security principal to prevent hidden-field tampering. Bean Validation is applied; on failure the form is re-rendered with inline error messages. On success, the user is redirected to the parcel's detail page.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the parcel being updated
      parcelDto - the form-bound updated parcel data; must satisfy all Bean Validation constraints
      bindingResult - holds validation errors produced during binding and validation
      model - the Spring MVC Model used to pass error messages and the today date attribute back to the view
      Returns:
      a redirect to /parcels/{id} on success, or the logical view name "parcel/form" when validation or persistence fails
      Throws:
      ResourceNotFoundException - if no parcel exists with the given ID

    • deleteParcel

      @PostMapping("/{id}/delete") public String deleteParcel(@PathVariable Long id, org.springframework.web.servlet.mvc.support.RedirectAttributes redirectAttributes)
      Handles the deletion of a parcel booking and redirects appropriately.

      Attempts to delete the parcel identified by id. On success, a flash attribute successMessage is added and the user is redirected to the parcel list. If the parcel does not exist, the exception message is surfaced as a flash error. Any other unexpected exception also results in a redirect to the parcel list with a generic error flash message.

      Parameters:
      id - the unique identifier of the parcel to delete
      redirectAttributes - Spring MVC redirect attribute container used to carry flash messages across the redirect boundary
      Returns:
      a redirect to /parcels in all cases
